text:
The Heavens Arena arc (, Tenkū Tōgijō-hen) is the second story arc of the series and follows Gon and Killua as they journey to Heavens Arena to train and make money. In addition to encountering Hisoka, the two meet a man named Wing who agrees to teach them something called Nen.

Summary

Goodbyes for now

Kurapika explains his theory of how Gotoh performed his coin trick on Gon. Gon mentions wanting to return Ging's Hunter License to Kite, and also to return the punch that Hisoka gave him during the Fourth Phase of their Hunter Exam to finally return Hisoka's badge to him, but has no clue where to find Hisoka. Kurapika reveals that Hisoka told him during their fight that he would be in Yorknew City on September 1 st (more than six months away, where the largest auction in the world would be held). Having reunited with Killua, Kurapika then leaves to use his Hunter License to earn money and attend the auction. Leorio leaves to study for medical school, planning to use his Hunter License to pay for it. The group agree to reunite on September 1 st in Yorknew City. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 44 

Killua suggests training to Gon, and uses an exaggerated drawing to illustrate his perception of the difference between Gon and Hisoka to Gon. Seeing as they are both also running low on money, Killua suggests a way to deal with both training and cash flow - by going to the Heavens Arena, the fourth tallest building in the world with 251 floors, where people earn money for fighting. The duo use most of their remaining funds to buy tickets to go there. 

Progressing up Heavens Arena

Gon and Killua arrive at Heavens Arena, and see a long queue to enter. They eventually get to fill out their registration forms and step into a big arena with several rings, allowing for simultaneous fights. Killua explains that this is his second time there; his first time was at the age of six, when his father left him there without any money and told him to stay until he reached the 200 th floor, and took him two years to do so. 

Gon and Killua are soon separately called to fight. They win their fights and are sent to the 50 th floor. Killua also sees another boy younger than them get sent to the 50 th floor. The three take the same lift up and receive an explanation of the floor system while in the elevator. On the 50 th floor, the boy introduces himself as Zushi, part of the Shingen-Ryu school, and bows his head in admiration when he learns that Gon and Killua are self-taught. Wing steps towards them, congratulates Zushi for his battle, and introduces himself as Zushi's mentor to Gon and Killua. The three boys head to collect their prizes for their first fights. Killua lets them know that the prizes go up fast as they go up the floors. The other two boys are stunned into silence upon learning that Killua used his prize of 200 million Jenny from the 190 th floor on four years of snacks. They head to a waiting room to wait for a second match that day, expecting to be called to fight again that day. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 45 

Killua and Zushi soon are called to battle each other. The fight begins and Killua quickly closes the distance despite noticing Zushi's well-trained fighting stance. Zushi feels pressured and decides to give a simple punch, but Killua dodges and counterattacks with a chop to the back of his head, knocking Zushi to the ground and earning Killua two of the ten needed points to win the match. Zushi stands up, surprising Killua, who gives another chop to the back of his head, but gets no points this time as Zushi manages to recover faster than before. Unable to think of a way to deal with Killua, Zushi adopts a new stance, and focuses his energy. Killua finds himself instinctively jumping back. Suddenly, Wing shouts at Zushi from his seat in the stands, surprising Zushi, Killua, the commentator, and the surrounding spectators. 

As they progress through the floors, Killua tells Gon about a bad feeling he got from Zushi during their fight that reminded him of facing against Illumi. After making it to the 100 th floor, Gon and Killua decide to ask Zushi to explain it to them. Zushi recites a mantra about how to train for Nen: 'Learn "Ten", know "Zetsu" through "Ren", to attain "Hatsu"'. This explanation makes no sense to Killua or Gon, who recognize none of the terms Zushi has said. Wing overhears them and reminds Zushi that he is not yet qualified to each, then shares an old proverb to Gon and Killua to advise them against learning things in an improper manner. Killua explains his belief that "Nen" is the secret behind Illumi's powers, and asks Wing to teach it to him and Gon, promising to not seek other tutelage if Wing taught them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 46 

Wing brings them to his hotel room and explains "Nen" ( or ネン, Nen—lit. "Burn"; "Flame" in the Viz translation) as a power that inflames one's soul expressed by one's strength of will, and explains the four terms recited in Zushi's mantra as exercises to harness one's strength of will: 
"Ten" (, Ten; "Point"): Focus the mind, reflect upon the self, and determine the goal. 
"Zetsu" (, Zetsu; "Tongue"): Put it into words. 
"Ren" (, Ren; "Temper"): Intensify your will. 
"Hatsu" (, Hatsu; "Release"): Put it to action. 

Wing then proceeds to give a demonstration, asking Killua for permission to (express an intent to) kill him. Killua consents, and Wing begins slowly going through the four motions, and eventually expresses his Nen. Gon flinches and reflexively adopts a battle stance, and Killua breaks out into a cold sweat, jumping into a corner of the room. Wing concludes his demonstration by advising that it is best to begin learning "Nen" by learning "Ten". 

On their way home, Killua tells Gon that he suspects Wing lied to them by omission, not believing Wing's explanation was sufficient to explain things like Zushi's peculiar resistance. In Wing's hotel room, Zushi asks Wing why he lied to Gon and Killua, by pretending that homophones of the four "Nen" principles were the real Nen exercises. Wing explains his caution in choosing who he teaches Nen by tearing a page from one of Zushi's book, infusing it with Nen, and throwing it to slice through a juice can across the room. 

The 200 th floor

Gon and Killua eventually make it to the 200 th floor. As soon as they step out from the lift, they feel a heavy presence surrounding them, preventing them from passing entering the floor. A Heavens Arena employee shows herself and lets Gon and Killua know that they have less than 4 hours to register their eligibility. Gon and Killua remain tense, unsure if she is the source of the aura. Hisoka steps forward and explains that he stalked the two to Heavens Arena. He warns them that they are not yet ready for the 200 th floor and intensifies the aura against the two. Wing appears behind them and agrees with Hisoka. He apologizes for not being completely honest in his earlier explanation of Nen, and asks them to let him rectify his mistake. The employee warns them that failing to register in time would mean that Gon has to restart from the first floor and Killua would be barred from competing. Gon and Killua decide to take the risk and follow Wing to try and learn Nen in time instead of forcing their way through Hisoka's aura in their current state to register. Hisoka seats himself at the end of the hallway and waits to see if they return. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 47 

Wing takes them to his room and explains that Nen ( "Mind Force" in the Viz translation) is the ability to control the life energy, or "Aura", that suffuses one's body using the four major Nen principles: 

Ten (; "Envelop"/"Shroud"): Contains the normally-leaking life energy within one's body. 
Zetsu (; "Suppress"/"Null"): Shuts the flow of aura from one's aura nodes off. 
Ren (; "Refine"/"Enhance"): Produces more aura. 
Hatsu (; "Release"/"Act"): Transmitting one's aura into one's surroundings. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 48 

After demonstrating the potential of Nen by damaging a wall with it, Wing explains the two methods to learn Nen – slowly like Zushi did, which let a natural talent like Zushi master Ten in six months, or forcibly by using another's aura to open one's aura nodes, opening oneself to the risk of malicious and harmful aura. Wing explains that everyone on the 200 th floor uses Nen, and that any newcomers would be "initiated" into Nen by being attacked with it. 

Wing forces his aura into them and opens up Gon and Killua's aura nodes. Immediately, they see their aura leaking out of of them, like steam spewing from a kettle. They listen to Wing's instructions and instinctively master the flow of their Nen with just verbal directions. Wing mimics Hisoka's malicious aura, and the boys find themselves now able to stand their ground. They return to the 200 th floor and are welcomed back by Hisoka, who lets them skip the initiation. Hisoka tells Gon that he is still not ready for Hisoka to fight him, but tells Gon that he would reconsider if Gon wins a fight, then leaves. Gon and Killua see three figures step out of the shadows, and follow them as they head to the registration counter. The three fighters convince Gon to fight with them. Gon and Killua head to their rooms, and see that Gon has already been scheduled for a fight the next day. Gon tells Killua that he expects to lose, but wants to practice his Ten. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 49 

Gon vs. Gido

The next day, Gon faces off against Gido, one of the three figures. Gon immediately activates his Ten, and Gido brings out his Dancing Tops. He launches the tops at Gon, who cannot predict their motion, and Gon eventually discovers how strong they are when they bounce off each other and hit him in the back. Gon tries several ways to follow their movement but is continually hit, and eventually knocked out of the ring by the sheer force of them. From outside the ring, he notices that the tops are not targeting him, but rather simply anything in the ring, and charges back in with newfound confidence. Gon jumps at Gido, but Gido uses his Tornado Top to send Gon flying out of the ring a second time. Backed into a corner by the threat of losing via points, Gon releases his Ten, closes his eyes, and naturally progresses into a state of Zetsu, despite not having been taught it yet. Gon finds himself able to feel the tops and sense where they are going, and devotes all his resources to avoiding enemy attacks. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 50 

After the match, Killua visits Gon at a medical station. Gon's numerous broken bones have the doctors estimate he would take four months to fully heal. Killua berates Gon for his lack of consideration for his own safety in releasing his Ten. Wing pays a visit to Gon and admonishes him for his foolishness before expressing his gratitude that Gon had not been more seriously injured. After consulting Killua about the doctor's diagnosis, Wing forbids Gon from both participating in any matches and training or studying Nen, and ties a string onto Gon's left pinky finger to mark his pledge and serve as a reminder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 51 

Wing calls Killua aside and speaks with him about their goals in Heavens Arena. Killua shares that Gon says his goal is to fight Hisoka, but his fight yesterday spoke otherwise and showed that Gon was enjoying the thrill of the fight, having continued to perfectly dodge almost 50 of Gido's tops for almost an hour while in Zetsu. Wing hesitates for a moment about whether teaching Nen to Gon and Killua was the right thing to do, but decides to ask Killua to continue studying with Zushi. Killua declines, not wanting to get a head start on Gon, and Wing asks Killua to let Gon know to practice "Nen" and "Ten" daily. Killua returns to Gon's room to explain Wing's instructions, sees Gon meditating, and quietly joins him. Gon reflects about his journey so far, about his aunt, Kite and his hunt for Ging, his animal friends, and his interactions with Hisoka. 

In a separate location, Kurapika approaches a tattooed lady at an agency to find clients as his way of earning money and finding a way to the auction. The agent rejects Kurapika despite him owning a Hunter License since she judges that he is still unable to see Nen, and asks him to return when he can. 

Hisoka vs. Kastro

One month later, Killua visits Gon and is surprised to see him healed. Killua shows Gon tickets for a match between Hisoka and Kastro. Gon puts on his sling and they leave to watch. On the way, Killua tells Gon about Hisoka's near-perfect fighting record. Wing appears, surprising them, and forbids Gon from watching Hisoka's match. Killua decides to watch it alone and tape the fight for Gon. Gon returns to his room and looks at the promise string on his finger. Despite wanting to practice his Ten, he manages to restrain himself and meditates. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 52 

An hour before the match, Killua sneaks into the waiting area and approaches Kastro's room to try and judge Kastro's abilities for himself. Through an half-open door, he sees Kastro sitting on a couch. He gets closer and is about to open the door when he is surprised by Kastro approaching him from behind. He checks the room and sees that the couch is now empty, and quietly panics as he tries to figure out how it happened. Killua decides to sheepishly pretend that he wanted an autograph, but is surprised a second time to learn that Kastro knows him by name. He asks Kastro how he snuck up on him, but Kastro declines explaining in case he fights Killua in the future. 

Hisoka and Kastro's fight begins with Kastro thanking Hisoka for his "initiation". Kastro charges at Hisoka and attacks with what appears to be a simple swipe which Hisoka dodges, but Hisoka finds himself unexpectedly hit by it anyway. Kastro continues his barrage of attacks and Hisoka continues to unsuccessfully dodge. In the midst of the barrage, Hisoka notices Kastro appear to vanish or teleport. Kastro does not explain his technique to Hisoka, and instead prepares to use his Tiger Bite Fist against him. As Kastro charges towards him, Hisoka offers up his left arm for Kastro to take. Kastro feigns attacking Hisoka's left arm, but instead "teleports" and snaps off Hisoka's right arm below the elbow. Hisoka kicks Kastro away as his arm flies through the air. He catches his arm, and announces his conclusion that Kastro's ability is to create a Double, which Kastro confirms. Kastro explains more about his ability to Hisoka, and prepares to use the True Tiger Bite Fist. Hisoka bites his severed forearm, once again taunting Kastro into attacking him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 53 

Hisoka hides his severed right arm with a handkerchief. He throws the handkerchief into the air, and cards come fluttering down from beneath it. Hisoka tells Kastro to choose a number from a card, and asks what number would he get if he applied the following formula: (((? + 4) * 2 - 6) / 2) -?. From his severed elbow, Hisoka pulls out an ace, and tosses it to Kastro. Kastro knocks it away, and watches as his Double charges in. Kastro's Double severs Hisoka's remaining arm, but suddenly vanishes. Hisoka brandishes a functional right arm, surprising both Kastro and the spectators. Hisoka makes a prediction about Kastro's death to him, and worsens his mental stability. Kastro dismisses Hisoka and resummons his Double to flank Hisoka with, but Hisoka easily identifies the correct Kastro due to the grime on his clothes from their battle. Kastro jumps away and lets his Double fight Hisoka, but is taunted into charging in once again and is punched squarely in the jaw by Hisoka's severed left arm. Kastro's head rings, and his Double vanishes when he loses focus. The cards that Hisoka scattered across the ground come flying at him. Kastro tries to reproduce his Double to block, but is unable to focus enough to do so, and the cards impale themselves into Kastro's limbs. Kastro collapses, and Hisoka walks away the victor. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 6, Chapter 54 

 appears]]

After the fight, Hisoka walks through a dimly-lit corridor carrying his left arm. A woman steps out of the shadows and congratulates him on his win, before asking Hisoka to show her his arms. She asks Hisoka why he chose to let his arms get severed, and begins treating his wounds without waiting for a response. They begin with his left arm, and blood spurts out of his wounds when Hisoka turns off his aura. She stops the bleeding with a tourniquet of Nen and uses her Nen Stitches to reattach Hisoka's severed left arm to its stump. She does the same for his right arm, and asks for a payment of 20,000,000 and 50,000,000 for his left and right arms respectively. Hisoka hides the stitches using a combination of Bungee Gum and Texture Surprise. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 55 

The woman prepares to leave but passes a message to Hisoka before she does. She tells Hisoka that everyone (of the Spiders) must meet up in Yorknew City by noon on August 30 th , and warns him not to blow another summons off. Hisoka feigns fear at the threat and asks her to dinner, but hears the door slam behind him and sees her gone. 

Hisoka takes a shower, catches a reflection of his back in the mirror, and is reminded that he has a Spider tattoo on him. He removes it, and the Nen surrounding the "skin" vanishes, revealing the tattoo to be conjured using Texture Surprise. Having found new sources of entertainment in Gon and Killua, Hisoka prepares to hunt down the Spiders. 

Triple Threat

Another month passes, and Wing has Gon and Killua resume their studying with Zushi. Wing asks Gon to use his Ten, and the group see that Gon's Ten is quiet and gentle yet powerful. Wing explains that the improvement despite a lack of practice is due to Gon's diligence in practicing "Ten". Wing points out the string on Gon's finger, and Gon and Killua are surprised to see it broken. Wing explains that he had tied it using Nen such that it would break if Gon tried to use Nen. Wing continues their training by having the boys watch the recording of Hisoka's match, explains In, asks the two to practice Gyo, and has Zushi use Gyo to try and deduce Hisoka's ability. The boys ask what Hisoka's ability is, and if they could use it. Wing answers by explaining that they could use some form of ability just like Hisoka does, but their abilities would be shaped by their personalities and environments growing up and were unlikely to be like Hisoka's. Wing ends their session by having Gon and Killua prepare to fight as late as possible without being kicked out from the floor, and tasks them with mastering Gyo by then. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 56 

The boys leave Wing's room and head to Killua's room to review what they learned. While on the way there, they are intercepted by Gido and his two companions, who believe Gon and Killua to be easy prey. One of them begins to demonstrate his ability to get them to compromise and agree to a fight. The boys don't respond to the trio and leave for Killua's room where they continue practicing their Ten and Ren. 

After their practice, Zushi walks home and finds himself suddenly unable to move. One of Gido's companions uses his ability to grab Zushi with a hand made of aura. The other secures him when Killua appears and offers to give them each free wins in exchange for Zushi, so that Gon does not have to break another promise to Wing. They register to fight on May 29 th and Zushi is freed. As they leave, Gido phones Gon, and coerces him into fighting as well despite Killua's efforts. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 57 

The next day, Killua goes to Gon's room to head to Wing's room together but finds that Gon has already left. He heads down, and sees people selling tickets to Gon's fight with Sadaso. Killua gets a description of Sadaso and his ability from the ticket seller, confirming that Sadaso had broken his promise to not make Gon fight as well. At Wing's room, he sees Zushi is alright, albeit unaware of the previous day's events. Killua asks to see Hisoka's tape, and is able to successfully use Gyo and accurately see Hisoka's Bungee Gum in action. Wing is impressed that both him and Gon have learnt Gyo in one night, and asks if he too finished his assignment early to fight sooner. Gon and Killua both realize that the other is lying to Wing, both having chosen to fight to protect Zushi. Wing allows them to fight sooner, and advises them to continue their Nen practice until then. 

Two weeks later, Killua confronts Sadaso before their fight. He threatens to kill Sadaso for breaking his promise to not force Gon to fight, and Sadaso forfeits their match. Sadaso calls his companions from a train station, explaining what happened and giving them a warning about Killua. The other two listen to his call together and discuss what to do. Killua appears and threatens them as well, asking them to stick to their promise. Gon and Killua continue training and sparring with Zushi under Wing's tutelage until the day of their fights. Before their fights, Wing asks Gon and Killua how they feel. Gon shares his plan to use a fishing rod in his fight with Gido. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 58 

As soon as his rematch with Gido begins, Gon charges in. Gido responds by immediately using his Tornado Top defensively and uses Shotgun Blues to launch homing tops at Gon. Gon uses his Ren and takes the shots head on. Gon casts his fishing rod at Gido and his hook bounces off Gido, landing behind him, but Gon pulls his line back in and catches his hook on the stone tiles that make up the floor of the ring. Using his fishing rod, Gon pulls the stone tile that Gido is spinning on off the floor, and Gido stops spinning as he drops to the ground. Gon quickly closes the distance, and breaks Gido's steel peg leg in two with a Nen-reinforced punch, threatening to do the same to Gido's face should they touch Zushi again. Gon is declared the winner of their fight. Killua's fight with Riehlvelt begins shortly after. Killua begins the fight by jumping high into the air above Riehlvelt, but Riehlvelt uses Aura Burst to quickly accelerate his wheelchair out of danger. Riehlvelt unveils two snake-shaped whips and lashes them defensively, but Killua catches them. Riehlvelt is surprised but presses a button on the handles to send a large surge of electricity through them and electrify Killua. The electricity does not stop Killua and Riehlvelt finds himself launched into the air when Killua pulls on the whips. Killua catches Riehlvelt with the electrical whips and Riehlvelt is knocked out, making Killua the winner of their fight. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 59 

A few days later, Gon faces Riehlvelt in a fight. Killua watches their match, having won his match with Gido by Gido's forfeiting. Hisoka also watches Gon's match. Riehlvelt begins using his whips defensively as soon as the match begins, but Gon simply pulls up a stone tile and throws it at Riehlvelt. Riehlvelt uses Aura Boost to dodge it, but Gon's predicts this and jumps onto his wheelchair, grabbing and crushing his wrists. The whips wrap themselves around Riehlvelt's neck, and Gon feigns turning the electricity on. The fear is enough to make Riehlvelt pass out, and Gon is declared the winner. As Gon walks out of the ring, he sees Hisoka waiting for him in the corridor, who tells Gon to name a day and place for them to fight. 

The next day, Wing begins training the boys in Hatsu, having decided that they have mastered all the Nen basics. He gives them a basic overview of the six broad categories of Nen users, and explains how how these categories played a part into the techniques and battle outcomes of their fellow fighters on the 200 th floor. Wing demonstrates a water divination test, and the boys identify their Nen categories. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 60 

At Wing's instruction, the boys continue practicing their abilities for the next four weeks. During this time, Gon contacts Hisoka and sets a day to fight. They go to show the fruit of their efforts to Wing, who is impressed with the progress the boys have made. After they demonstrate their progress to Wing, Wing congratulates Gon for passing the secret Hunter Exam, explaining that Nen was required to be a full-fledged hunter. Wing elaborates on several other facts, including why Nen and this Hunter Exam were a secret, that Netero was the grand master of the Shingen-Ryu school, and that he had been planning to teach them all along. He also shares the progress of some of the other successful examinees from the 287 th Hunter Exam. Wing and Zushi see Gon and Killua off. Wing reassures Zushi that he is a rare talent, despite Gon and Killua being even more rare talents. 

Gon vs. Hisoka

The next day, Gon fights Hisoka. The audience cheer loudly as Gon appears from one door and Hisoka from the other shortly after. The announcer introduces them as they walk to the ring. They meet, and observe the others' Ten. Hisoka notices Gon's determined and confident look, and gets aroused at the thought of fighting Gon. From the audience, Wing wishes Gon well, observing Hisoka's ominous aura. Killua also watches their match from the stands. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 61 

The referee announces the beginning of their fight, and Gon begins by jumping towards Hisoka to launch a barrage of punches. Hisoka easily dodges and finds an opening for him to counterattack. He kicks Gon away, who recovers and jumps back to continue the barrage, until Hisoka lands a clean hit on Gon. 

Gon takes a moment to reevaluate as the referee awards Hisoka a point. Hisoka taunts Gon by pointing out that he has not yet moved from his starting position. Gon charges in and feigns another barrage, before jumping a short distance back and throwing a stone tile at Hisoka. He jumps forward as the stone flies toward Hisoka; he shatters it mid-air with a kick and uses the debris to hide himself. Hisoka manages to spot him, but cannot react fast enough, and Gon gives him a clean punch to the face. 

The referee awards two points to Gon for the hit. Gon and Hisoka approach each other, and Gon returns Hisoka's badge to him. Hisoka accepts the badge, and asks Gon about his Nen progress, correctly guessing Gon's Nen type through a personality test he developed. Hisoka takes the initiative this time and launches a barrage of attacks at Gon, who only manages to dodge and block some of them. Gon takes another moment to evaluate from a distance as the referee awards Hisoka more points. Seeing that Gon is not approaching, Hisoka activates his Bungee Gum to draw Gon in. Gon realizes what is happening too late and uses Gyo to see Hisoka's aura stuck to his cheek. Hisoka explains the ability to Gon as he launches another barrage of unavoidable attacks on Gon. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 62 

Gon decides to try and take the initiative and jumps towards Hisoka. Hisoka sees Gon's expression as he charges in and gets even more aroused. He lets Gon get several good punches in so that the fight can be prolonged. He enjoys the punches Gon gives him, but eventually contracts his Bungee Gum which pulls Gon directly into his fist. Gon endures another set of unavoidable attacks, but is surprised to hear the referee award Hisoka more points despite defending. The crowd boos at the referee's decision, and Gon braces himself, now at risk of losing from another hit. Hisoka points to Gon's right, and tells him to look to the right, but Gon is hit from his left in the face by a piece of debris, drawn to him by Hisoka's Bungee Gum. The referee declares Hisoka the winner, and Gon sits in disbelief as Hisoka walks away. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 7, Chapter 63 

As he walks away, Hisoka tells Gon that he lacked experience, and that their next fight would be somewhere outside Heavens Arena, a life or death fight without rules or points. Gon watches Hisoka walk away and cannot help but feel the distance between their combat abilities. He resolves to learn more about Nen and find an ability to rival Hisoka's. 

After the fight, Killua finds Gon, and asks to go to his place since the two have already met all their goals there. They say goodbye to Heavens Arena and head to Gon's home, his first visit in seven months. 

Notable Characters

Major Fights

Story Impact

The idea of Nen is introduced in full, though many minor details are only specified later on.
Gon and Killua learn the basics of Nen from their teacher Wing. 
The secret Hunter Exam hinted at during the Hunter Exam Arc is revealed. Successful examinees of the Hunter Exam are only considered full Hunters once they have discovered the existence of Nen and successfully learnt it.
Gon passes the secret Hunter Exam under Wing's tutelage.
When Wing passes Gon, he also reveals the status of their peers from the 287 th Hunter Exam in learning Nen.
Hanzo and Kurapika have learned Nen from other teachers
Illumi and Hisoka knew Nen before their Hunter Exam
Leorio plans to begin after applying to medical school
Pokkle is struggling with Ren
Kurapika also learns Nen from Izunavi. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 30 (2011) 
Gon is able to land a punch on Hisoka and return his badge from the Hunter Exam arc. 
A female member of the Phantom Troupe is introduced. 
Hisoka is revealed to be masquerading as a Troupe member. 
Kurapika reveals at the start of the arc that Hisoka said he would be at Yorknew City on September 1 st after somehow learning about Kurapika's interest in the Phantom Troupe. After Hisoka's fight with Kastro, it is confirmed that all members of the Phantom Troupe would be at Yorknew City on September 1 st . This includes Hisoka, whose status as a member of the Phantom Troupe has been made ambiguous by the revelation that his Spider Tattoo is faked with his Texture Surprise.

Trivia

Two scenes of Wing’s calendar in the 2011 anime adaptation place the events of this arc chronologically during the months of March to July 2000. This is done by showing the pages for June and July; in both cases, the correspondence between dates and days of the week matches the year 2000 instead of 1999. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 33 (2011) Hunter × Hunter - Episode 35 (2011) This is, however, contradicted in the manga during later arcs.

Anime and Manga differences

 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Mito receives a letter from Gon and is seen reading it with her grandmother in the ultimate episode. The letter details Gon's adventures during the 287 th Hunter Examination and includes a picture of Gon, Killua, Leorio, and Kurapika. The contents of this episode are not present at all in the manga. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 26 (2011) 
Curiously, despite the picture being taken after Gon's reunion with Killua, he does not mention his time after the exam heading to Kukuroo Mountain, and simply mentions going to Heavens Arena.
In the manga, as the group discuss what to do after Killua's return, Gon also shares that he wishes to find Kite to return the Hunter License Kite dropped while protecting a young Gon from a foxbear attack. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 1 , which is not mentioned in the 2011 anime adaptation. 
 
----
All differences:
In the 2011 anime adaptation, the scene where Gon, Killua, Leorio, and Kurapika discuss their plans after having left Kukuroo Mountain is included in the first season's penultimate episode instead of the second season (the first season adapts the Hunter Exam arc; the second season adapts the Heavens Arena arc).
In the 2011 anime adaptation, the group have their discussion on what to do after Killua's return in several areas, including in the small town outside Kukuroo Mountain, where the group boarded a tour bus to Killua's home, through the train in the Dentora Region of the Republic of Padokea, outside a train station exit in the town that the group first landed in when heading to Kukuroo Mountain, and inside the airport. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 25 (2011) In the manga, they have their discussion in entirety in the town with the train station exit. 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, when Kurapika explains Gotoh's coin trick, he explains it from Gotoh's perspective ("[Gotoh] held two coins"). In the manga, Kurapika explains it from his perspective ("I had two coins"). 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Gon says that he won't use his Hunter License or go back to Whale Island until he returns Hisoka's badge to him. In the manga, Gon says that he doesn't feel like he has earned his license until he returns Hisoka's badge to him, and does not mention Whale Island. 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, while explaining what Hisoka said to him during their battle in the Final Phase of the 287 th Hunter Exam, Kurapika mentions that the Phantom Troupe are bandits. In the manga, he simply mentions that they will be at the auction, without hinting at their profession. 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Kurapika specifies searching for a patron to earn money to participate in the Southernpiece auction. In the manga, he specifies using his Hunter License to earn money to participate in the auction. 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, when explaining his perception of the difference in combat skill level between Gon and Hisoka, Killua draws Hanzo on Hisoka's right. In the manga, Killua draws Hanzo on Hisoka's left. 
As a result, the meaning of Gon's question: "Hanzo's stronger?" could be implying "stronger [than Killua] in the 2011 anime adaptation and implying "stronger [than Hisoka]" in the manga. Since the full drawing is not shown, either implication is possible.
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Killua asks Gon to follow him to Heavens Arena without explaining what it is then, and instead explains it on the flight there. In the manga, Killua explains what it is and where it is in an internet cafe before they book their tickets there. 
In the manga, Killua whispers to Gon to put down 10 years' worth of combat experience to help boost his initial ranking. This scene is not present in the 2011 anime adaptation. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 27 (2011) 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Zushi is the one to introduce the fact that fighters who reach the 100 th floor are given their own rooms. He uses that as a way to interject into the conversation and introduce himself. In the manga, Killua is the one to introduce this fact, and Zushi does not introduce himself until they step out of the elevator. 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Zushi's betting odds in his match against Killua's are weighted at 1.508 (Zushi) and 2.075 (Killua). In the manga, these odds are 1.500 (Zushi) and 2.075 (Killua). 
In the manga, Killua is seen starting to perspire during his fight with Zushi after knocking him down twice, whether due to wariness or exertion. In the 2011 anime adaptation, Killua does not visibly perspire until Zushi prepares to use Ren. 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, the announcer for Killua and Zushi's fight explains that a spectator's shout had interrupted their fight. In the manga, the announcer passes it off as the crowd cheering loudly. 
In the 2011 anime, Wing approaches Gon and Killua on an escalator to prevent them from watching Hisoka's match. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 31 (2011) 
In the 2011 anime, Gon says goodbye to Wing and Zushi, but in the manga, they just leave. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 36 (2011)
